headline: Trump Says Weapons Makers Agree to Expand Production
summary: President Trump said America’s largest weapons makers have agreed to raise production of military munitions following “a very good meeting” at the White House on Friday.  Executives from BAE Systems, Boeing, Honeywell Aerospace, L3Harris Technologies, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman and RTX attended Friday’s meeting, Trump said.  “They have agreed to quadruple Production of the ‘Exquisite Class’ Weaponry in that we want to reach, as rapidly as possible, the highest levels of quantity,” Trump wrote in a social media post.

raw.headline: Trump Says Weapons Makers Agree to Expand Production
raw.summary: President Trump said America’s largest weapons makers have agreed to raise production of military munitions following “a very good meeting” at the White House on Friday. headline: Trump to Quadruple Missile Production. What Global Rearmament Means for Defense Stocks.
summary: Rising global military spending—from the U.S., Europe, and China—could fuel growth for defense contractors and reshape manufacturing and employment in the years ahead.

headline: Trump Says Defense Primes Agree To Quadruple Output of 'Exquisite Class' Weaponry
summary: President Trump says he secured commitments from major defense contractors to "quadruple Production of the ‘Exquisite Class' Weaponry."

headline: Trump says defense CEOs agree to quadruple production of 'Exquisite Class' weaponry
summary: President Trump said he met with CEOs of BAE Systems, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, RTX Corp., Boeing, Honeywell Aerospace and L3Harris Missile Solutions.

headline: Embraer's Fourth-Quarter Earnings and Revenues Surpass Estimates
summary: EMBJ beats Q4 estimates with $2.65 billion in revenues and strong jet deliveries as defense, executive aviation and services growth help lift the backlog to $31.6 billion.

raw.headline: Embraer's Fourth-Quarter Earnings and Revenues Surpass Estimates
raw.summary: EMBJ beats Q4 estimates with $2.65 billion in revenues and strong jet deliveries as defense, executive aviation and services growth help lift the backlog to $31.6 billion.

headline: CFOs On the Move: Week ending March 6
summary: Delta’s CFO moves to the COO role, L3Harris names a new finance chief as it prepares to spin off its missile business and McKesson hires Sysco’s CFO.
